title: Chionolaena chrysocoma
taxonomy: Chionolaena chrysocoma is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Galapagos Islands. The plant is classified within the genus Chionolaena, which is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as eudicots. The specific classification for Chionolaena chrysocoma is as follows: Chionolaena chrysocoma, Asteraceae, Campanulales, eudicots, angiosperms, and plants. Further classification puts it in the kingdom Plantae.

The plant's leaves and stems are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and digestive issues. Chionolaena chrysocoma is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help in preventing infections and promoting wound healing. However, more scientific research is needed to confirm its medicinal uses and potential side effects.
